eishin.description: The other party elevates your abilities, and you naturally improve around them. This is the most ideal mutual growth combination in Sukuyodo.
eishin.detailed: Eishin represents the highest compatibility. Both parties develop positively when together, creating a virtuous cycle of mutual elevation in both professional and personal contexts.
eishin.advice: The relationship quality is high, but it still requires active maintenance. Express gratitude regularly.
avoid: Taking the other's contributions for granted
Neglecting the relationship because it feels effortless

gyotai.description: Deep intuitive understanding from the start. You can anticipate each other's thoughts with minimal communication. A past-life karma bond.
gyotai.detailed: Gyotai represents karmic connections from previous lives. The gyo party drives the other's mission; the tai party receives nurturing. Collaboration efficiency is naturally high.
gyotai.advice: Leverage the natural rapport but don't skip formal communication.
tips: Use natural rapport to quickly establish collaboration
avoid: Relying solely on intuition without verification
gyotai.description_classic: Gyotai: karmic bonds from past lives.

mei.description: Mirror-image compatibility. Nearly identical thinking and behavior patterns. Zero communication barriers but limited complementarity.
mei.detailed: Mei means same mansion, same essence. Understanding each other is effortless, but shared blind spots are a risk.
mei.advice: Enjoy the frictionless communication but actively seek external perspectives.
avoid: Both diving into the same blind spot

yusui.description: You invest energy to make the other comfortable, but reciprocation is uneven. Works for short-term collaboration; watch for drain in the long run.
yusui.detailed: In yusui, the yu party provides social energy while the sui party enjoys it with limited return. The yu party gradually tires.
yusui.advice: If you're the yu party, set clear boundaries. If sui, consciously reciprocate.
avoid: Unlimited giving by the yu party
good_for: Roles with clear division of labor
yusui.description_classic: Yusui: yu side auspicious for socializing, sui side for recovery only.

kisei.description: Complementary skills and perspectives. Requires an adjustment period, but once past it, this becomes a highly efficient partnership.
kisei.detailed: In kisei, the ki party faces pressure that drives growth, while the sei party strengthens through refinement. Initial discomfort is normal.
kisei.advice: Give the adjustment period enough time. Initial friction doesn't mean incompatibility.
avoid: Giving up due to initial discomfort

ankai.description: The kai party disrupts the an party's stability. Stimulating but draining. Short-term growth is possible, but long-term risk is high.
ankai.detailed: Ankai is the highest-tension relationship in Sukuyodo. The an party continuously outputs energy to maintain stability while the kai party draws from it.
ankai.advice: If you're the an party, set firm boundaries. External balancing mechanisms are essential.
tips: Short-term collaboration is safer than long-term
avoid: Long-term deep commitment without external support
ankai.description_classic: Ankai: the kai side 'nothing else is bearable.'
